title: Dialogue 1: At a Cafe
setting: Marie enters a small cafe in Paris.

lines.speaker: Serveur
lines.french: Bonjour, madame ! Vous desirez ?
lines.english: Hello, ma'am! What would you like?

lines.speaker: Marie
lines.french: Bonjour ! Je voudrais un cafe et un croissant, s'il vous plait.
lines.english: Hello! I would like a coffee and a croissant, please.

lines.speaker: Serveur
lines.french: Tres bien. Un cafe creme ou un cafe noir ?
lines.english: Very good. A latte or a black coffee?

lines.speaker: Marie
lines.french: Un cafe creme, s'il vous plait.
lines.english: A latte, please.

lines.speaker: Serveur
lines.french: Voila, madame. Ca fait trois euros cinquante.
lines.english: Here you are, ma'am. That's three euros fifty.

lines.speaker: Marie
lines.french: Merci beaucoup ! C'est delicieux.
lines.english: Thank you very much! It's delicious.

title: Dialogue 2: Asking for Directions
setting: Lucas is lost near the train station.

lines.speaker: Lucas
lines.french: Excusez-moi, ou est la gare, s'il vous plait ?
lines.english: Excuse me, where is the train station, please?

lines.speaker: Passant
lines.french: C'est tout droit, puis tournez a gauche.
lines.english: It's straight ahead, then turn left.

lines.speaker: Lucas
lines.french: C'est loin d'ici ?
lines.english: Is it far from here?

lines.speaker: Passant
lines.french: Non, c'est a cinq minutes a pied.
lines.english: No, it's five minutes on foot.

lines.speaker: Lucas
lines.french: Merci beaucoup, monsieur !
lines.english: Thank you very much, sir!

lines.speaker: Passant
lines.french: De rien, bonne journee !
lines.english: You're welcome, have a good day!

title: Dialogue 3: Meeting a New Classmate
setting: First day at a French class in Luxembourg.

lines.speaker: Sophie
lines.french: Bonjour ! Je m'appelle Sophie. Et toi ?
lines.english: Hello! My name is Sophie. And you?

lines.speaker: Tobi
lines.french: Salut ! Je m'appelle Tobi. Enchanté.
lines.english: Hi! My name is Tobi. Nice to meet you.

lines.speaker: Sophie
lines.french: D'ou viens-tu ?
lines.english: Where are you from?

lines.speaker: Tobi
lines.french: Je viens du Nigeria. Et toi ?
lines.english: I come from Nigeria. And you?

lines.speaker: Sophie
lines.french: Je suis luxembourgeoise. Tu parles francais ?
lines.english: I'm Luxembourgish. Do you speak French?

lines.speaker: Tobi
lines.french: Un peu ! J'apprends le francais depuis trois mois.
lines.english: A little! I've been learning French for three months.

lines.speaker: Sophie
lines.french: Tres bien ! On peut pratiquer ensemble.
lines.english: Very good! We can practice together.

title: Dialogue 4: At the Doctor's Office
setting: Paul doesn't feel well and visits the doctor.

lines.speaker: Client
lines.french: Bonjour, ou sont les fruits, s'il vous plait ?
lines.english: Hello, where are the fruits, please?

lines.speaker: Employe
lines.french: Au fond du magasin, a gauche.
lines.english: At the back of the store, on the left.

lines.speaker: Client
lines.french: Merci. Et le fromage ?
lines.english: Thanks. And the cheese?

lines.speaker: Employe
lines.french: Rayon trois, juste apres le pain.
lines.english: Aisle three, just past the bread.

lines.speaker: Client
lines.french: Est-ce que les tomates sont en promotion ?
lines.english: Are the tomatoes on sale?

lines.speaker: Employe
lines.french: Oui, un euro le kilo aujourd'hui.
lines.english: Yes, one euro per kilo today.

lines.speaker: Client
lines.french: Parfait, j'en prends deux kilos.
lines.english: Perfect, I'll take two kilos.

title: Dialogue 5: Shopping for Clothes
setting: Emma is looking for a new dress.

title: Dialogue 6: At the Hotel Reception
setting: Checking into a hotel in Nice.
